title: Ipomopsis stenothyrsa
taxonomy: Ipomopsis stenothyrsa is classified within the family Polemoniaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the skyrocket or grierson's globemallow. This family is further classified under the order Polemoniales. The genus Ipomopsis, to which Ipomopsis stenothyrsa belongs, is native to western North America. Ipomopsis stenothyrsa is a species of flowering plant. It is a perennial herb with showy flowers.

intro: Ipomopsis stenothyrsa, also known as scarlet gilia or cardinal gilia, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its vibrant red flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relieve congestion and soothe the respiratory tract. The bioactive constituents of Ipomopsis stenothyrsa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ipomopsis stenothyrsa is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat upper respiratory tract infections, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation.
